zh.Bath Abbey, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特巴斯 艾比
zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Cullen於1571年1月1日在英國薩默塞特巴斯 艾比受洗時，她的父親William Cullen III26歲，母親Jone Millard20歲。她於1595年4月16日與Lord Mayor Matthew Randall在英國薩默塞特巴斯 艾比結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她埋葬在英國薩默塞特巴斯 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Cullen于1571年1月1日在英国薩默塞特巴斯 艾比受洗时，她的父亲William Cullen III26岁，母亲Jone Millard20岁。她于1595年4月16日与Lord Mayor Matthew Randall在英国薩默塞特巴斯 艾比结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她埋葬在英国薩默塞特巴斯 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Cullen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Declan, Dermot, Donal, Fergus, Aidan, Brennan, Donovan, Eamon, Eamonn, Kieran, Liam.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cuileáin ‘descendant of Cuileán’, a variant of the personal name Coileán meaning ‘puppy’ or ‘young hound’ (see Collin ).
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cuilinn ‘descendant of Cuileann’, a byname meaning ‘holly’.

variants.name: Collin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Leonide, Andre, Armand, Camille, Emile, Fernand, Firmin, Francois, Ghislaine, Normand.
English, French, Walloon, and Breton: variant of   Colin  . This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
German: variant of   Kollin   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Cullinan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Cormac.
Irish (Clare): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cuileannáin ‘descendant of Cuileannán’, from a diminutive of Cuileann (see Cullen 2).

variants.name: Cullens
meanings: Scottish: variant of Cullen , with excrescent or genitival -s.
Dutch (Rotterdam): possibly an adaptation of English   Collins  .

variants.name: Cullin
meanings: Irish (Longford and Roscommon) and English: variant of   Cullen  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Cullen was christened on 1 January 1571, in Bath Abbey, Somerset, England, her father, William Cullen III, was 26 and her mother, Jone Millard, was 20. She married Lord Mayor Matthew Randall on 16 April 1595, in Bath Abbey, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 1 daughter. She was buried in Bath Abbey, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Cullen a été baptisée le 1 janvier 1571, à Bath Abbey, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, William Cullen III, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Jone Millard, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Lord Mayor Matthew Randall le 16 avril 1595, à Bath Abbey, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 1 fille. Elleenterrée à Bath Abbey, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Cullen am 1. Januar 1571 in Bath Abbey, Somerset, England getauft wurde, war ihr Vater William Cullen III 26 und ihre Mutter Jone Millard 20. Sie hat am 16. April 1595 in Bath Abbey, Somerset, England Lord Mayor Matthew Randall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 10 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie wurde in Bath Abbey, Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Cullen è stato battezzato il 1 gennaio 1571 nel Bath Abbey, Somerset, England, sua padre, William Cullen III, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Jone Millard, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Lord Mayor Matthew Randall il 16 aprile 1595, nel Bath Abbey, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 10 figli e 1 figlia. Fu sepolta a Bath Abbey, Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Cullenが1571年1月1日にイギリスソメルセトバス アビーで洗礼を受けた時，父William Cullen IIIは26歳，母Jone Millardは20歳であった。1595年4月16日にイギリスソメルセトバス アビーでLord Mayor Matthew Randallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスソメルセトバス アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Cullen(이)가 1571년 1월 1일 영국 서머세트 바스 애브브이에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Cullen III(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jone Millard(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1595년 4월 16일 영국 서머세트 바스 애브브이에서 Lord Mayor Matthew Randall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 서머세트 바스 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.1 January 1571–19 February 1627: 1 de janeiro de 1571–19 de fevereiro de 1627
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Cullen foi batizada em 1 de janeiro de 1571, em Bath Abbey, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Cullen III, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Jone Millard, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Lord Mayor Matthew Randall em 16 de abril de 1595, em Bath Abbey, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 1 filha. Ela foi sepultada em Bath Abbey, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Cullen была крещена 1 января 1571 г. в местности бат эбби, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени William Cullen III было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Jone Millard, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lord Mayor Matthew Randall 16 апреля 1595 г. в местности бат эбби, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она была похоронена в местности бат эбби, сомирсет, Англия.

es.1 January 1571–19 February 1627: 1 de enero de 1571–19 de febrero de 1627
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Cullen se bautizó el 1 de enero de 1571, en Bath Abbey, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, William Cullen III, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Jone Millard, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Lord Mayor Matthew Randall el 16 de abril de 1595, en Bath Abbey, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 1 hija. Fue sepultada en Bath Abbey, Somerset, Inglaterra.
